highlights: Architected a two-tier JSON storage system on ClickHouse for native in-depth JSON log queries, handling ~100M logs/minute in SigNoz Cloud production.
Automated forwarding of AWS-generated S3 log files into SigNoz by building the S3 Sync feature.
Owned the Logs Pipelines feature end to end, improving JSON parsing and integrating the OpAMP protocol with the SigNoz OTel Collector
Expanded SigNoz's AWS integration suite by onboarding 5 new services - ECS, DynamoDB, SNS, ElastiCache, and SQS.
Designed and delivered a full data-engineering stack end to end - ingestion, warehouse, transformation, and BI - as the founding backend engineer.
Cut full-load ingestion time 12X and CDC 2X by designing Gear5, a Go-based data replication system that later became the foundation of olake.io

Cut warehouse scale-up/down from 5–8 min to <90s via OpenEngine - storage/compute separation on AWS Fargate / Azure Container Apps with live query rerouting to freshly provisioned ClickHouse instances.
Enabled analysts to author models, track data lineage, and schedule transformation jobs by building a transformation layer on top of dbt-core (OSS).
Automated cluster lifecycle management with CL-Master, an operator handling node up/down-scaling, rolling image updates, system recovery, warehouse EBS autoscaling, and license management.
Built wrapper APIs for OCR-based document processing and a bulk URL-shortening tool (Excel-driven) for internal platform use.
Shipped CLI commands for mesheryctl to manage filters, patterns, and performance profiles/results, including the ability to run performance tests directly from the CLI.
Raised mesheryctl test coverage by 33% by introducing Go unit testing for its Cobra commands.
Built a pre/post-deployment health-check system to validate Meshery's environment state.
Refactored and consolidated ~65 server endpoint handlers and authored OpenAPI documentation covering ~70 handlers for Meshery's REST API.
